definition: Prisoner's dilemma dynamics drive food export ban cascades during FX-driven food price spikes. 33 countries restricted exports in 2007-08; 20+ in 2022. Each restriction is individually rational but collective restrictions amplify original price shock by 30-60%. Conditional probability >60% given simultaneous USD strength >10% and wheat >$8/bu (n=3).
evidence: 30-60% amplification of original price shock from collective restrictions
Game-theoretic framing acknowledged as elegant by debate
10% global supply shortfall can produce 30-40% price spikes
Simultaneous food inflation and currency pressure for import-dependent countries
open_questions: Individual country responses are politically idiosyncratic
Interaction with current WTO enforcement mechanisms
Can WTO mechanisms or bilateral agreements prevent contagion?
Does the mechanism strengthen or weaken as global trade integration increases?
Have post-2022 food security frameworks reduced the tail risk?
What is the threshold for export ban cascades?
How does this interact with EM credit spreads quantitatively?
